lifecycles._note: Light, self-contained ROLE lifecycle covering 7 role write ops. A real role create->read->update->bindings->delete is attempted in group 'role-real'; every step is optional so an environmental 500/400/403 (the prior disabled iam-role saw POST /v1/roles -> 500 ContactAdminForAssistance) records coverage on the CALL and skips the rest of the group cleanly rather than failing the run. Bodies grounded on api_bodies.json (createrole/setrole/setroletrustpolicy/addrolepolicybindings). assume_role_policy_document uses an empty Resource and no real Principal (principals[] empty) to avoid referencing a non-existent user SRN. policy_ids sent empty on create; the binding steps then add/remove a placeholder policy id (synthetic -> expected 400/404). The standalone group 'role-bulk' exercises DELETE /v1/roles/bulk and the bindings DELETE endpoints against ids that need no real role (bulk delete of an empty/synthetic set), so those writes are CALLED even if role create 500s. RISK (from docs, not runtime-proven): create role may 500 on this account; bulk role delete body shape (role_ids) is from convention, not a confirmed example -> _note on that step. Create response envelope $.role.id confirmed from createrole 202 doc example.

steps.name: set-role-trust-policy
steps._note: Body from setroletrustpolicy; Principal/Resource minimized (empty Resource, sts:AssumeRole only) to avoid a real-SRN dependency.

steps.name: add-role-policy-bindings
steps._note: addrolepolicybindings: synthetic policy id -> expected 400/404 (no real policy bound) but the POST is CALLED.

steps.name: remove-role-policy-binding
steps._note: {unique} stands in for the synthetic policy id added above; remove single binding (path param segment normalizes to '*' for catalog match).

steps.name: remove-bulk-role-policy-bindings
steps._note: removebulkrolepolicybindings: detaches all policy bindings from the role.

steps.name: delete-roles-bulk
steps._note: Standalone (does NOT need role-real to succeed) so DELETE /v1/roles/bulk is CALLED regardless. Body shape role_ids is from convention (no confirmed example) -> synthetic id, expecting 400/404/422.

lifecycles._note: Light. Covers policy write ops NOT covered by the base iam-policy lifecycle: PUT /v1/policies/{policy_id}, PUT /v1/policies/{policy_id}/bindings, DELETE /v1/policies/bulk, plus the user/group policy-binding add/remove and resource-policy statement ops. A real USER_DEFINED policy is created (group 'pol-real', body from createpolicy with server-assigned id fields omitted) then set/bound/deleted; all sub-steps optional so a 4xx records coverage. The standalone group 'pol-bulk' exercises DELETE /v1/policies/bulk against a synthetic id so it is CALLED even if create fails. Create envelope is FLAT $.id (confirmed from createpolicy 201 doc example, same as base iam-policy).

steps.name: set-policy
steps._note: setpolicy: server-assigned version id fields omitted; updates name/description/document.

steps.name: set-policy-bindings
steps._note: setpolicygroupbinding: add/remove a synthetic GROUP id -> expected 400/404 but PUT is CALLED.

steps.name: delete-policies-bulk
steps._note: Standalone so DELETE /v1/policies/bulk is CALLED regardless. Body policy_ids from convention (no confirmed example) -> synthetic id, expecting 400/404/422.

lifecycles._note: Light. Covers group member + policy-binding write ops not in the base iam-group lifecycle: POST/DELETE /v1/groups/{group_id}/members, POST/DELETE /v1/groups/{group_id}/policy-bindings. Creates a real group (group 'grp-bind'; body from creategroup with empty principals so no pre-existing user/policy needed), then adds a synthetic member + policy binding and removes them. All sub-steps optional: a synthetic user/policy id returns 400/404 but the POST/DELETE is CALLED. Group create envelope $.group.id confirmed from creategroup 201 doc example.

steps.name: add-group-member
steps._note: addgroupmember: synthetic user_id -> expected 400/404, POST is CALLED.

steps.name: add-group-policy-binding
steps._note: addgrouppolicybinding: synthetic policy id -> expected 400/404, POST is CALLED.

lifecycles._note: Light, standalone (no resource create). Covers POST/DELETE /v1/users/{user_id}/policy-bindings against a synthetic user id so both writes are CALLED. A synthetic user id yields 400/404 (user not found) but coverage is recorded on the call. adduserpolicybinding body from api_bodies.json (policy_ids).

Light, standalone. Covers the resource-policy write ops: PUT/DELETE /v1/resource-policies/{srn}, POST /v1/resource-policies/{srn}/statements, PUT/DELETE /v1/resource-policies/{srn}/statements/{sid}. A resource policy attaches to an existing resource by its SRN; we use a synthetic SRN ({srn} literal) so the API returns 400/404 (resource not found / invalid srn) but every write is CALLED. Bodies from setresourcepolicy (server-assigned version id fields omitted) and addpermission/setpermission. The SRN is a single path segment containing ':' which normalizes to '*' for catalog matching. RISK: real resource-policy ops require a valid target resource SRN the test account owns; not runtime-proven here.

steps.name: add-resource-policy-statement
steps._note: addpermission: a single statement object.

steps.name: set-resource-policy-statement
steps._note: setpermission: replace a single statement identified by sid (path param segment normalizes to '*').

lifecycles._note: HEAVY (irreversible/credential + account-user + SAML ops). Runs only under SCP_RUN_HEAVY. Covers: POST/PUT/DELETE /v1/access-keys (+ bulk + send-otp); POST/PUT/DELETE /v1/accounts/{account_id}/users (+ bulk) and PUT .../password; POST/PUT/DELETE /v1/saml-providers (+ bulk). These create real credentials / users / federation providers, so each is optional with a broad expect_status: on a shared account most return 403 (permission) or 400 (body/account scope) which still records coverage on the CALL. {account_id} uses a synthetic id; access-key/saml ids likewise synthetic for the PUT/DELETE-by-id steps. createsamlprovider/setsamlprovider bodies in api_bodies.json are corrupt (_raw stringified) -> we send a best-effort multipart-less JSON guess; marked NOT runtime-proven. accesskeycreate parent_access_key_id/passcode omitted (account-specific). RISK: every step here is unproven against a real 2xx; the goal is purely to CALL each write endpoint so a future live run records it.

steps.name: access-key-send-otp
steps._note: accesskeysendtemporaryotp: sends an OTP to the caller; safe-ish but gated heavy because it triggers a real notification.

steps.name: create-access-key
steps._note: accesskeycreate: PERMANENT key. parent_access_key_id/passcode omitted. Captures id if returned; the PUT/DELETE-by-id steps fall back to a synthetic id when capture misses (separate group).

steps.name: set-access-key
steps._note: accesskeyset: enable/disable. Synthetic id ({unique}) so the PUT is always CALLED; path param segment normalizes to '*'.

steps.name: delete-access-keys-bulk
steps._note: Body access_key_ids from convention (no confirmed example) -> synthetic id.

steps.name: create-account-user
steps._note: createiamuser: empty group_ids/policy_ids; synthetic account_id ({unique}) -> expected 400/403/404, POST CALLED. user_name unique.

steps.name: update-account-user
steps._note: updateiamuser.

steps.name: set-account-user-password
steps._note: updateiamuserpassword. password omitted-as-empty to avoid setting a weak real password; expecting 400/403.

steps.name: delete-account-users-bulk
steps._note: Body user_ids from convention (no confirmed example) -> synthetic id.

steps.name: create-saml-provider
steps._note: createsamlprovider: api_bodies.json entry is corrupt (_raw). Best-effort JSON guess (name + metadata_document placeholder); NOT runtime-proven. Likely 400 on invalid metadata, which still records coverage.

steps.name: set-saml-provider
steps._note: setsamlprovider: corrupt body in api_bodies.json; best-effort guess; synthetic id.

steps.name: delete-saml-providers-bulk
steps._note: Body saml_provider_ids from convention (no confirmed example) -> synthetic id.
